channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: NHL's Capitals and NBA's Wizards are staying in Washington after Virginia arena deal collapses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Ted Leonsis told District of Columbia Mayor Muriel Bowser late last year that the NBA's Washington Wizards and NHL's Washington Capitals he owns would probably be leaving Washington for Virginia, she told him no, they would not. Ultimately, she proved to be right.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, SARAH RANKIN and MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yankees get infielder Jon Berti from Marlins in 3-team trade
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Jon Berti could be at third base for the banged-up Yankees in their opener against Houston on Thursday after New York acquired the infielder as part of a three-team trade with Miami and Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sinner advances to 3rd Miami semifinal in 4 years; Alexandrova continues run by ousting Pegula
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Australian Open champion Jannik Sinner won his 20th match of 2024 to reach the Miami Open semifinals for the third time in the past four years with a 6-4, 6-2 victory over Tomas Machac on Wednesday. Sinner, seeded second, will take on either No.
item.dc:creator: AP, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legendary college football coach Nick Saban unveils major lifestyle changes since retiring
item.description: Nick Saban admitted he is settling into retirement, telling ESPN his primary stress now centers around keeping his grandson away from hazards when he's on the golf course.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Warriors' Draymond Green is ejected less than 4 minutes into game against Magic
item.description: Golden State forward Draymond Green was ejected less than four minutes into the Warriors' game against the Orlando Magic on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: AP, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Draymond Green ejected less than 4 minutes into game after jawing at officials
item.description: One night after a hard foul in Miami, Draymond Green was ejected less than four minutes into the Warriors' game in Orlando on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Athlete of the Week: White Plains High School senior Logan McCormick
item.description: WHITE PLAINS, N.Y. (PIX11) -- The offseason. A chance to use what you learned on the court to be better for the next year. Few players take better advantage of the time than White Plains High School's Logan McCormick.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: Nashville Predators are 16-0-2 since their trip to see U2 in Vegas was canceled
item.description: Barry Trotz watched his Nashville Predators lose a game at home 9-2, their second consecutive defeat and a third poor effort in a row and he decided they'd be better without a trip to see U2 perform at the Sphere in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers CEO Jed York ready and willing to pay Brock Purdy ahead of looming contract extension
item.description: San Francisco 49ers CEO Jed York knows the value he has in quarterback Brock Purdy and on Tuesday he vowed when the time comes, the organization is prepared to pay up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bees ready to open final season at Smith's Ballpark
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Salt Lake Bees are about to open its 30th season in downtown Salt Lake City, and its final year at Smith's Ballpark. The Bees kick off the 2024 season at Sacramento Friday with its home opener set for Tuesday against Tacoma.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: A’s fan boycott: Public safety concerns grow as A’s plan to restrict parking lot access
item.description: Oakland Vice Mayor Rebecca Kaplan is asking the A’s to move up the opening of their parking lot from 5 p.m. “and not harm community health and safety by creating a backup.
item.dc:creator: Jason Mastrodonato, The Mercury News

item.title: Ex-Buccaneers tight end, Rob Gronkowski, visits veterans in Tampa
item.description: The Super Bowl Champion and former tight end for the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, Rob Gronkowski, paid a visit to veterans in Tampa on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bay FC sells out inaugural home game Saturday, traffic delays expected
item.description: (KRON) -- After winning its first match 1-0 on the road against Angel City FC, the Bay Area's new women's professional soccer team, Bay FC, will play its first home game Saturday. On Wednesday, the team announced that the match was sold out.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Phillies-Braves and Brewers-Mets openers postponed a day to Friday due to rainy forecasts
item.description: Baseball season openers in New York and Philadelphia have been postponed a day until Friday because of rainy forecasts in both cities.
item.dc:creator: Mike Fitzpatrick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Baseball 2024: What’s it like to sing the national anthem at an A’s or Giants game?
item.description: Whether you're 8 years old, a professional slack-key guitarist or dressed like a "Star Wars" character, singing the national anthem is always an unforgettable experience.
item.dc:creator: Jim Harrington, The Mercury News

item.title: New York Mets 2024 home opener postponed due to expected rain
item.description: FLUSHING, N.Y. (PIX11) -- The New York Mets 2024 home opener has been postponed due to rain expected in the forecast, the team announced on Wednesday. The game originally scheduled for Thursday against the Milwaukee Brewers will now happen on Friday at 1:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Rizk, PIX 11

item.title: The hottest tickets in baseball this season are in Los Angeles
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers made some major investments over the winter, committing more than $1 billion to a pair of Japanese superstars, but it appears it won't take long for the club to recover the costs.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Washington State’s AD vacancy: Six names to watch as the Cougars start the search to replace Pat Chun
item.description: The Cougars need candidates who are willing to accept the risks involved and understand life at schools in remote locations with limited resources.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Opening day 2024: What to watch for on the first full day of the MLB season
item.description: After the Dodgers and Padres soft-launched the 2024 MLB season with a two-game series in South Korea last week, the rest of the league was scheduled to start play Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Report: Bears president calls lakefront stadium plans chance for city to have 'a crown jewel' at NFL owners meetings
item.description: CHICAGO — Weeks after saying the franchise is prepared to make a massive financial commitment to building a domed stadium on the lakefront, Chicago Bears President and CEO Kevin Warren has touted the plan at the annual NFL owners meetings.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Former Gov. Pat Quinn: Ask the voters if they want public funding for Bears, White Sox stadiums
item.description: CHICAGO — Former Illinois Gov. Pat Quinn has a simple message for City of Chicago officials when it comes to public funding for new stadiums: Ask the voters.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Racist abuse prompts Utah to change hotels in women's NCAA tourney
item.description: The University of Utah women’s basketball team was forced to move hotels after the team experienced several “racial hate crimes” while traveling for the NCAA March Madness tournament.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Utes 'deeply troubled and shaken' by racist incident
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The University of Utah athletics department released a statement Tuesday saying the team is "deeply troubled and shaken" by a racist incident that occurred during their stay in Coeur d'Alene, Idaho for the NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Aztec fans, including 'Show Grandmas,' gear up for Sweet 16
item.description: San Diego State Aztec fans are ready to continue cheering on their team into the Sweet 16 on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Alani Letang, Fox 5

item.title: Tony Finau out to win second Houston Open
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - 2024 has not been the kind of year that Tony Finau was expecting, but the Salt Lake City native is hoping to turn that around this week. "It's always a work in progress, and I know on any given week bound to get hot and and be in contention," Finau said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Angels provide first look at new food offerings for upcoming '24 season
item.description: The Los Angeles Angels are preparing for Opening Day of the 2024 MLB season, and on Tuesday, the team gave KTLA's Orange County Bureau Chief Chip Yost an exclusive look at the new food offerings fans can expect at Angel Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Chip Yost, KTLA 5

item.title: Idaho officials apologize to Utah women's basketball team after 'racial hate crimes,' U of U responds
item.description: City officials in Coeur D’Alene, Idaho, apologized to the Utah Women’s Basketball Team on Tuesday for the racist incidents that happened while the team was staying in the city for the NCAA tournament.
item.dc:creator: Jonathon Sharp, ABC 4

item.title: Moose on the Loose: Shot in the arm
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Sometimes athletes surprise you with their performance, and Knicks guard Donte DiVincenzo has certainly done that. He was drafted in the first round, 17th overall, of the 2018 NBA Draft after helping the Villanova Wildcats win a national championship.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Sports betting hits college campuses as students admit to using financial aid to place bets
item.description: According to the Nevada Council on Problem Gambling, one in five college students have admitted to using their financial aid money to place bets.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shohei Ohtani says he has 'never' gambled on sports
item.description: The MLB star spoke publicly for the first time since allegations of theft and gambling opened an investigation into his interpreter.
item.dc:creator: Urja Sinha, NewsNation

item.title: Historic USF men’s basketball season comes to end in NIT 2nd round
item.description: The historic season for the University of South Florida men’s basketball team comes to an end in the NIT second round.
item.dc:creator: Chad Smith, USF associate director of communications, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bracket not busted: All top 8 seeds advance to Sweet 16 in chalky March Madness
item.description: March Madness arrived with visions of chaos. Based on last year's bracket, there was little reason to doubt it. The only surprise so far has been the lack of pandemonium. The top two seeds from each region are headed to the Sweet 16 for just the fifth time. One double-digit seed will join them.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stanford heads to Sweet 16 after OT win over Iowa St.
item.description: STANFORD, Calif. (AP) — Kiki Iriafen hit midrange jumpers from every possible spot. She took the ball hard at the hoop and right at star Iowa State freshman Audi Crooks, then blocked Crooks' path on the defensive end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bulldog Insider feature: softball honors Amanda Scott and Donna Pickel
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE) – Fresno State Athletics honored two Bulldog softball icons on Friday evening at Margie Wright Diamond. Inaugural head coach Donna Pickel, and 4x All-American and National Champion pitcher Amanda Scott.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Bulldog Insider feature: shortstop Ben Newton
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE) –Fresno state senior shortstop Ben Newton has played a consistent role in the Bulldogs' lineup. Batting sixth he provides a spark when he's up to bat. Newton credits the Bulldogs' success this season to their offseason work and veteran group.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Bulldog Insider feature: Fresno State Pro Day
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE) – 20 NFL scouts were in attendance at Fresno State's Pro Day last week. Eleven draft-eligible players took part in drills. Former Bulldog DB Carlton Johnson was the only Bulldog invited to the NFL Scouting combine. He injured his hamstring and was unable to participate.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Colorado STUNS Florida on last-second shot
item.description: An absolutely wild back-and-forth high-scoring affair ended in perfect March Madness fashion - with a last-second bucket for the win.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
